2605.01874.title: Leveraging Data Symmetries to Select an Optimal Subset of Training Data under Label Noise
2605.01874.paper_summary: The performance of machine learning models often relies on large labeled datasets; however, data collected from diverse sources can contain label noise. Recent work has shown that, in noisy settings, there may exist a subset of the training data on which models can achieve performance comparable to training on a noise-free dataset. A widely used method for identifying such subsets is cutstats, which employs k-nearest neighbors (k-NN) to detect low-noise samples. However, its performance on high-dimensional data remains largely unexplored. In this work, we formally establish that the performance of a classifier trained on a subset of a noisy dataset selected via cutstats is influenced by the accuracy of k-NN. We further demonstrate that, in noisy environments, exploiting data invariance and knowledge of underlying symmetries can significantly enhance the performance of k-NN, bringing it closer to the Bayes optimal classifier even in high-dimensional regimes. Finally, we show that for real-world scenarios, where information about the underlying invariance is only partially known, learnt invariant representations can still facilitate the identification of near-optimal subsets.
2605.01874.paper_summary_zh: 機器學習模型的性能通常依賴於大型標記數據集；然而，來自不同來源的數據可能包含標籤噪聲。最近的研究顯示，在噪聲環境中，可能存在一個訓練數據的子集，使得模型的性能可以與在無噪聲數據集上訓練的性能相當。識別這類子集的一種廣泛使用的方法是 cutstats，它利用 k-nearest neighbors (k-NN) 來檢測低噪聲樣本。然而，它在高維數據上的性能仍然未被充分探索。在這項工作中，我們正式確立了通過 cutstats 選擇的噪聲數據集子集上訓練的分類器的性能受到 k-NN 準確性的影響。我們進一步證明，在噪聲環境中，利用數據不變性和對潛在對稱性的知識可以顯著提高 k-NN 的性能，使其即使在高維環境中也更接近貝葉斯最佳分類器。最後，我們展示了在現實世界場景中，當對潛在不變性的資訊僅部分已知時，學習到的不變表示仍然可以促進近似最佳子集的識別。
2605.01874.author: Kumar Shubham et.al.
2605.01874.authors: Kumar Shubham, Pavan Karjol, Kiran M K, Prathosh AP
